original_hypothesis.title: TierCalculationLib.prizeCount(4**tier) may overflow for unexpected tier values, cascading into prize count/fee computations
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: TierCalculationLib.prizeCount computes `4 ** _tier` on uint256. While protocol bounds tiers to <= 14/15 in most places, any path that calls prizeCount with an out-of-range tier (e.g., external misuse, future code changes, or invalid inputs in view helpers) could overflow and revert, causing DoS in dependent computations.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a correctness footgun: the library itself has no defensive bounds. Given other functions accept tier/numTiers from external callers, ensure there is no reachable state-changing path where an attacker can force an invalid tier into prizeCount and revert critical operations.
preconditions: A reachable code path supplies _tier large enough to overflow 4**_tier on uint256, or violates MAXIMUM_NUMBER_OF_TIERS constraints.
The reverting computation is used inside a state-changing function.

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: unchecked exponentiation on user-provided / externally influenced tier
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: overflow revert DoS if tier bounds ever bypassed

analysis_questions: Are there any state-changing paths where `_tier` is not validated against numberOfTiers before calling prizeCount/prizeCount-derived functions?
Could numberOfTiers ever be corrupted outside [3,15]?
validation_requirements: Global grep: all TierCalculationLib.prizeCount call sites and their tier validation
Fuzz invalid tier inputs to PrizePool.claimPrize and related paths (should revert earlier)

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If any future/alternate entry point uses prizeCount without validating tier bounds, attacker supplies large tier to force revert and block operations.
prerequisites: Tier bounds bypass (not evident in current claim path due to _checkValidTier)

potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Users attempting to claim/close draws via affected path

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No fuzzing shown for invalid tier overflow behavior at library level

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
